KeyFeatureCase1.sectionTitle: Why GPT Image 2 Is Different From Every AI Image Tool You've Tried
KeyFeatureCase1.subtitle: Whether you are a designer, marketer, or solo creator, GPT Image 2 delivers a practical feature set that is powerful and easy to use.

items.title: Perfect Text Inside Images
items.text: Every other AI image model treats text as an afterthought. Signs come out garbled. Posters look like ransom notes. GPT Image 2 was rebuilt from scratch to render readable, properly placed text on the first try - headlines, buttons, labels, and even mixed-language layouts.
items.bestFor: Best for: Event posters, product banners, social graphics, UI wireframes, promotional materials.

items.title: Reasoning Before Generating
items.text: GPT Image 2 does not just match your words to pixels. It reasons through your request: understanding spatial relationships, planning the composition, and verifying accuracy before the first pixel renders. Ask it to generate a poster for a current event and it will look up the details in real time.
items.bestFor: Best for: Storytelling, product visualization, scene construction, character design.

items.title: Multilingual Visual Content
items.text: Working across global markets? GPT Image 2 natively supports Japanese, Korean, Chinese, Hindi, Bengali, and all Latin-script languages - rendered correctly inside images, not translated after the fact. Build one visual campaign that works everywhere.
items.bestFor: Best for: Graphic novel artists, UX storyboarders, brand campaign creators.

items.title: Production-Ready Resolution
items.text: Generate high-resolution visuals for posters, thumbnails, UI mockups, and product concepts. Paid plans may include higher-resolution export or upscaling options depending on availability. No watermarks. Outputs are royalty-free and cleared for commercial use under OpenAI's content policy.
items.bestFor: Best for: Teams testing multiple creative directions quickly.

items.title: Image-to-Image Editing
items.text: Upload any reference photo and describe what you want to change. GPT Image 2 edits with surgical precision - swap backgrounds, change colors, localize designs, or remove objects while preserving everything else exactly as-is.
items.bestFor: Best for: Concept prototyping, fast ideation, and creative exploration loops.

items.question: What is Wan 3.0 and how is it different from Wan 2.7?
items.answer: Wan 3.0 is the third major generation of Alibaba's open-source Wan video generation model series. Unlike Wan 2.7, which maxed out at 1080p and 16-second clips, Wan 3.0 supports native 4K resolution, 60fps output, and videos up to 60 seconds long. More significantly, Wan 3.0 introduces a neural physics engine for realistic object behavior, synchronized audio generation, and cross-shot character consistency that holds up through full multi-shot sequences. It's not an incremental patch — the model architecture has been substantially redesigned.

items.question: Is Wan 3.0 free to use?
items.answer: Yes — Wan 3.0 has a free plan that includes 5 generations per month at up to 1080p resolution. Free-plan videos include a watermark and are for personal, non-commercial use only. For unlimited 4K generation, no watermark, and commercial use rights, a Pro plan is available. The underlying model weights are also free to download and self-host under an open-source license. If you want to test output quality before subscribing, the free plan is the right starting point.

items.question: Can I use Wan 3.0-generated videos for commercial projects?
items.answer: Yes. All paid Wan 3.0 plans include full commercial use rights. Videos generated on the Free plan include a watermark and are licensed for personal, non-commercial use only. If you're producing content for clients, running ads, monetizing a YouTube channel, or any other revenue-generating use case, a Pro or API plan is required. The open-source model weights also permit commercial use with attribution — check the LICENSE file in the GitHub repository for specifics.

items.question: Does Wan 3.0 support image-to-video generation?
items.answer: Yes. Wan 3.0 accepts a reference image as input alongside (or instead of) a text prompt. You can provide a product photo, a character illustration, a landscape shot, or any other visual reference, and the model generates video that maintains the visual identity of that image while adding natural motion, lighting changes, and camera movement. This is especially useful for e-commerce product videos and brand-consistent content where you need the generated footage to match existing visual assets.

items.question: Is Wan 3.0 open-source? Where can I download the model?
items.answer: Yes — the core Wan 3.0 model weights are publicly available on Hugging Face and GitHub under a license that permits commercial use with attribution. You can download them, run them on your own GPU hardware, fine-tune them on custom datasets, and integrate them into your own products. The cloud platform at wan30.co is a managed, hosted version of the same model, optimized for speed and ease of use without any infrastructure overhead.

items.question: Does Wan 3.0 generate audio along with the video?
items.answer: Yes. Wan 3.0 includes a native audio generation capability conditioned on the visual content of the video — not a separate model applied after the fact. When you enable audio generation, the model produces sound that matches the visual scene: ambient environment noise, object impact sounds, movement audio, and atmospheric texture. You can also generate the video without audio if you prefer to add your own soundtrack in post-production. Audio generation is available on Pro and API plans.

items.question: How do I write effective prompts for Wan 3.0?
items.answer: Wan 3.0 responds well to prompts that describe four things: the subject, the action, the environment, and the camera. A prompt like "a barista pouring latte art in a sunlit coffee shop, close-up, slow motion, shallow depth of field" will produce a more precise result than "coffee shop video." Lighting conditions (golden hour, overcast, neon), camera movement (dolly in, handheld, aerial), and surface detail (wet pavement, rough concrete, polished marble) all influence the output. For image-to-video prompts, focus on describing the motion rather than the appearance, since the appearance is anchored by your reference image.

items.question: What are the system requirements to run Wan 3.0 locally?
items.answer: To run Wan 3.0 on your own hardware, you need a CUDA-compatible NVIDIA GPU with a minimum of 24GB VRAM for 1080p generation. For 4K output, 40GB+ VRAM is recommended (NVIDIA A100 or H100). The model supports both single-GPU and multi-GPU setups. A Docker image is available for simplified deployment. Full system requirements, setup instructions, and a configuration guide are in the Wan 3.0 GitHub repository. If local hardware isn't available, the managed cloud platform runs the same model without any setup required.

items.question: Why do AI-generated video characters look different between shots? Does Wan 3.0 fix this?
items.answer: Character drift — where a person's face, clothing, or surroundings change slightly (or dramatically) between generated shots — is one of the most common problems in AI video. It happens because most models treat each video generation as a fresh inference with no persistent memory of the character's visual identity. Wan 3.0 addresses this with a structural identity preservation mechanism that tracks key visual attributes (facial structure, clothing, hair, environment) across cuts. In sequences up to 60 seconds, Wan 3.0 maintains character and scene consistency through wide shots, medium shots, and close-ups without drift.

TestimonialGridCase1.subtitle: Real GPT Image 2 workflows: fast text-to-image generation, poster-ready layouts, and structured infographic visuals for ads, social, and product teams.

items.contentLines:
Introducing ChatGPT Images 2.0

A state-of-the-art image model that can take on complex visual tasks and produce precise, immediately usable visuals, with sharper editing, richer layouts, and thinking-level intelligence.

Video made with ChatGPT Images

gpt-image-2 is now available to everyone.

i tried generating the same image using nano banana pro and gpt-image-2.

the difference is clear, it is way better than nb pro.

here are the results:

gpt-image-2 vs nb pro

This image below reveals an artifact problem with GPT Images 2 that you can see in many generations:

There are dots everywhere. The bananas have dark dots on them, the fire above the pan has bright dots, and even the sugar has some kind of dotted pattern.

OMG, GPT Image 2 is insanely GOOD

It can generate 100 completely unique pixel art items with labels… in ONE shot

This is the first time image gen feels production-ready

It's rolling out for everyone

(Examples below)

Speed is livestreaming Grand Theft Auto 6

by GPT Image 2

🚨 GPT-Image 2 is rolling out now on ChatGPT!

Here's an easy prompt to test if you have it or not as V2 is the ONLY model that can do this:

"an ingame screenshot of rust"

Left image = GPT-Image 1.5 (Rust/CoD/Tarkov mix)
Right image = GPT-Image 2 (Literally Rust)

TestimonialGridCase2.title: What Creators Are Saying About Wan 3.0
TestimonialGridCase2.description: Wan 3.0 is being used by content creators, agencies, and indie filmmakers across the US. Here's what some of them have shared.

items.quote: The physics in Wan 3.0 genuinely surprised me. I generated a pour shot for a coffee brand — the liquid moved exactly how you'd want it to. I would have paid a production crew $800 for that shot.
items.author: Marcus T.

items.quote: I produce explainer videos for SaaS companies. Wan 3.0 cut my production time in half. The 60-second multi-shot capability is the thing that actually made it useful — I can generate a full narrative sequence without the characters falling apart.
items.author: Priya S.

items.quote: We tested every major AI video tool before committing. Wan 3.0 won on output quality and open-source flexibility. The API integration took less than a day to set up, and we've been running it in production for three months.
items.author: Jordan W.
items.role: CTO at a boutique creative agency

items.quote: I run a one-person studio and Wan 3.0 lets me deliver campaign variants in hours. I can match brand moodboards quickly and still keep motion believable.
items.author: Olivia R.

items.quote: The prompt adherence is strong enough for ad testing. We generate 10-15 hooks per week and iterate faster than our old edit pipeline.
items.author: Daniel M.

items.quote: For product shots, reflections and small object motion are much more stable than what we saw in other tools. That consistency matters for ecommerce.
items.author: Nina K.

items.quote: I teach editing workshops and students understand scene construction faster when they can prototype ideas in Wan 3.0 before touching a timeline.
items.author: Thomas L.

items.quote: We use it for pre-visualization before live shoots. It cuts alignment time with clients because they can approve pacing and framing early.
items.author: Maya C.

items.quote: The multilingual prompt quality is better than expected. Our EU team uses the same workflow and output quality stays consistent.
items.author: Luca P.

items.quote: I replaced expensive stock footage for explainer intros. The turnaround is fast enough to publish the same day for trend-based content.
items.author: Sarah J.

items.quote: Scene continuity over longer clips made the difference for us. We can actually chain shots without everything resetting stylistically.
items.author: Jake N.

items.quote: I generate fitness demo backgrounds and b-roll in batches now. It saves us from repeating outdoor shoots every week.
items.author: Amanda F.

TestimonialGridCase3.sectionTitle: What Users Say About Seedream 5.0 AI Image Generator

testimonials.name: Sarah Chen
testimonials.content: Seedream 5.0 has revolutionized our content creation process. We now generate stunning marketing visuals in minutes that used to take our design team hours to create. The quality is exceptional, and our social media engagement has increased by 40%.

testimonials.name: Michael Torres
testimonials.content: As a creative director, I'm always looking for tools that can help my team work faster without sacrificing quality. Seedream 5.0 delivers on both fronts. The AI's understanding of context and ability to generate on-brand visuals is impressive.

testimonials.name: Emily Watson
testimonials.content: Seedream 5.0 has been a game-changer for my online store. I can now create professional product images without expensive photoshoots. The consistency across all my product listings has improved my brand perception and increased sales.

testimonials.name: James Wilson
testimonials.content: As a content creator, visuals are everything. Seedream 5.0 helps me create thumbnails and Image graphics that stand out. The real-time web search feature ensures my visuals are always current and relevant to my content.

testimonials.name: Lisa Zhang
testimonials.content: I use Seedream 5.0 as a tool to generate ideas and speed up my workflow. It's not a replacement for my skills, but it's an invaluable assistant that helps me explore more creative directions and deliver projects faster to my clients.

HowToStepsCase1.sectionTitle: How to Use GPT Image 2 - 3 Simple Steps
HowToStepsCase1.subtitle: Using GPT Image 2 on gptimage.tools requires no technical background, no model downloads, and no complex setup.

steps.name: Step 1: Describe Your Vision
steps.text: Type your prompt in plain English. Include subject, style, and any exact text you want in the image.

steps.name: Step 2: Choose Your Settings
steps.text: Choose aspect ratio and quality based on your output target: social, banner, UI mockup, or print draft.

steps.name: Step 3: Generate and Download
steps.text: Generate, refine in natural language, and download your final watermark-free image in seconds.
